description: Dealer net positions in Treasury and other fixed income securities 1990–2020. Panel A shows primary dealers’ net spot positions in U.S. Treasury bills and coupon-bearing securities by week. Panel B compares primary dealers’ net positions in Treasury coupon-bearing securities with those in other fixed income securities (agency MBS, agency debt, corporate debt, and municipal debt), with correlations reported in the legend. The data is from July 4, 1990 to December 30, 2020.

Dealer Treasury spot and futures positions 1990–2001. This figure compares spot and futures net positions of primary dealers in U.S. Treasury bills (Panel A) and coupon-bearing securities (Panel B) by week from July 4, 1990 to June 27, 2001.

Treasury issuance and redemptions 1990–2020. Panel A (B) plots average weekly auction amounts (average weekly redemptions) of U.S. Treasury bills and coupon-bearing securities by quarter for the weeks ending July 4, 1990 to December 30, 2020.

Impulse response functions of Treasury inventory and net financing. This figure plots the generalized impulse response functions (GIRF) of Treasury inventory and net financing based on the vector autoregression (VAR) model of Treasury inventory and net financing changes with exogenous control variables (Treasury issuance, redemptions, changes in Federal Reserve holdings, and changes in foreign central banks’ holdings). The optimal lag length is chosen by the Bayesian Information Criterion (13 lags for the VAR model of bill inventory and net financing, and 14 lags for that of coupon inventory and net financing). The sample period is from July 4, 1990 to December 30, 2020. The GIRF is computed using a one-standard-deviation shock in the shocked variable.

Coupon auction purchases by investor class 2001–2020. This figure plots the 13-week moving average of the fraction of weekly coupon auction amounts purchased by broker-dealers, investment funds, and other investor classes as reported in the investor class allotment data from the Department of the Treasury.
